events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON. Need parse scenario. Residential job, data/comm & security, electrical scope. Observed field condition: untwisted pairs at termination >½ in. Work staged. Need select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong. Condition categories include installed-defective. Untwisted pairs at termination >1/2 inch is a defect in data cabling (CAT5/6) because TIA-568 requires maintaining twist within 0.5 in (13 mm) of termination point to avoid crosstalk/EMI. NEC 800 covers communications circuits. Since work staged, not begun, component condition installed-defective? Actually observed field condition maybe existing termination? It says staged, materials ready but work not begun. Could be pre-existing terminations observed. The observed choice wrong: leaving pairs untwisted >1/2 in at termination. Correct approach: re-terminate using proper punch-down tool and maintain pair twist within 1/2 inch of termination; use correct RJ45/keystone/jack and cable management. Decision fail because non-compliant. Risk maybe medium (performance issues, not safety). Work_stage staged. percent_complete maybe 0 or 10. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. value 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. workable empty. findings: untwisted pairs exceed 0.5 in at termination, violates TIA-568. actions: re-terminate maintaining twist within 1/2 inch, verify with cable tester. rationale: TIA-568 specifies max untwist length 0.5 in to preserve impedance and reduce crosstalk; NEC 800 requires communications circuits installed per manufacturer instructions/listing. references: TIA-568-C.2 or D, NEC 800. Need ensure JSON valid. Need include all fields. Let's craft.
